text:
French President Emmanuel Macron is bolstering security over France, with one expert claiming ISIS is looking at two major events in his calendar to plan an attack.

The upcoming French election could be one,or when the Olympic Games in Paris get underway, an expert tells the Daily Express. Lucas Webber, research fellow at TheSoufanCenter, a body that specialises in global security issues, says ISIS leaders have been called on by their supporters to carry out "assassination attempts".

Speaking to the Daily Express, he said: "ISIS-K and other Islamic State-backing groups have been calling for assassinations of world leaders and other political figures. There is a risk of this in France ahead of the election.

"As for the Olympics. Due to the drone threats that ISIS-K has repeatedly made, France has requested air defence systems from Greece and they have requested Starlink support from the UK.

"France is taking this drone threat seriously. ISIS-K is putting out instructional booklets on how to arm drones with munitions.

READ MORE:Emmanuel Macron accused by Michel Barnier of edging France closer to Frexit



"The Olympics is at high risk. France is another hub of increasing ISIS-K activity. After the Moscow attack, Macron said at least two plots were foiled in France in the year leading up to that.

"It looks quite bleak and high risk in France." Earlier this month,ISIS issued a threat to Francewith an image that depicted drone strikes on the country.

In the image, a drone is seen flying near the Eiffel Tower carrying a package that says 'gift' on it.

In March, French authorities reduced the size of the crowd that will be allowed to attend the Paris 2024 Olympic Games opening ceremony, which will take place on the River Seine.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Don't miss:ISIS releases chilling image urging 'lone wolf attacks' during Paris Olympics

Don't miss:Paris Olympics terror attack foiled by police as football stadium targeted

Don't miss:Warning issued that Britain's ‘unprepared’ for ISIS 'drone attack plot'



Around 300,000 spectators will be allowed to attend, down from the 600,000 that were previously expected.

The move was taken to ensure people are safe amid threats from terror groups.

Politico reported at the time that a senior official in the interior ministry said that President Macron personally demanded the crowd reduction due to security fears.

The top official said French intelligence services had not identified plans for terror attacks yet, but added: "The main threat is Islamist terrorism.”

title: ISIS is earmarking two major events in France for 'attack' as security is ramped up | World | News | Express.co.uk
author: Charlie Bradley
description: EXCLUSIVE: An expert tells the Daily Express that 'there is a high risk' of ISIS attacks ahead of the Olympic Games and French elections.
